quote_of_the_day.q: You'll see it when you believe it.
quote_of_the_day.h:
“You'll see it when you believe it. ” —
Wayne Dyer

random_quotes.q: It is the power of thought that gives man power over nature.
random_quotes.h:
“It is the power of thought that gives man power over nature.” —
Hans Christian Andersen

random_quotes.q: Confidence comes not from always being right but not fearing to be wrong.
random_quotes.h:
“Confidence comes not from always being right but not fearing to be wrong.” —
Unknown

random_quotes.q: The roots of education are bitter, but the fruit is sweet.
random_quotes.h:
“The roots of education are bitter, but the fruit is sweet.” —
Aristotle

random_quotes.q: No man is more cheated than the selfish man.
random_quotes.h:
“No man is more cheated than the selfish man.” —
Henry Ward Beecher

random_quotes.q: Don't bother people for help without first trying to solve the problem yourself.
random_quotes.h:
“Don't bother people for help without first trying to solve the problem yourself.” —
Colin Powell

random_quotes.q: The ability to hold two competing thoughts in one's mind and still be able to function is the mark of a superior mind.
random_quotes.h:
“The ability to hold two competing thoughts in one's mind and still be able to function is the mark of a superior mind.” —
F. Scott Fitzgerald

random_quotes.q: Courage is resistance to fear, mastery of fear - not absence of fear.
random_quotes.h:
“Courage is resistance to fear, mastery of fear - not absence of fear.” —
Mark Twain

random_quotes.q: Cherish forever what makes you unique, cuz you're really a yawn if it goes.
random_quotes.h:
“Cherish forever what makes you unique, cuz you're really a yawn if it goes.” —
Bette Midler

random_quotes.q: Not how long, but how well you have lived is the main thing.
random_quotes.h:
“Not how long, but how well you have lived is the main thing.” —
Seneca
